codeReview.summary: Cache-Control middleware is wired correctly and path coverage is broad, but it unconditionally stamps cache headers on all GET responses (including 4xx/5xx). This can cache transient negative responses for resources that are later created, so the feature does not safely satisfy cacheability expectations.

issues.description: `add_cache_control` applies Cache-Control based only on request method/path and does not gate on response status. As a result, 404/401/429 GET responses are cacheable (lines 114-121). This is problematic for endpoints like GET /api/videos/{id}/transcript and GET /api/videos/{id}/summary that legitimately return 404 before POST /ensure creates data (content.rs:46,167; main.rs:395-421), causing stale negative caching and delayed visibility of newly generated content.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-backend-worker skill mandates a strict RED step ('confirm new tests FAIL') that is awkward for new pure helper modules where tests and implementation are introduced together; this caused a nominal RED step that immediately passed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill text: .factory/skills/rust-backend-worker/SKILL.md step 5. Worker transcript shows 'run tests to see fail' followed by 'All 28 tests pass' in the same session entry (worker-transcripts.jsonl line 3). Handoff also flags this in skillFeedback.suggestedChanges (handoff file lines 158-159).

summary: Reviewed commit 12c8baf and transcript for backend-cache-headers. Implementation introduces comprehensive path-based cache directives and middleware wiring, but a blocking correctness issue remains: transient error responses are cached for GET endpoints, which can serve stale 404/unauthorized states after underlying data changes.
